summary: Good audience recording captures a solid 1990 show with standout Estimated Prophet, Terrapin Station and an emotional Touch of Grey.
review: The audience tape is praised as clear and surprisingly high‑quality for a coliseum AUD, letting the grand piano work of Brent/Hornsby shine through. The performance is generally solid – fans highlight powerful versions of Estimated Prophet, Terrapin Station and a heartfelt Touch of Grey, though one reviewer calls it one of the weaker 1990 shows overall.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating (≈3) combined with consistently positive comments on recording quality and several standout song performances, tempered by a few negative remarks about overall show strength.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape noted as very good/ great; clear enough to hear piano, drums and space jams despite typical coliseum ambience. No major drop‑outs or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlist described as classic for the era with solid song selection; emotional high point on Touch of Grey and a notable final version of Stander, though overall performance considered uneven by at least one reviewer.

band_member_comments.Keys: Grand piano effect praised; Bruce Hornsby’s keyboards noted as a huge addition that lifted the band.
band_member_comments.Drums: Sikiru Adepoju adds percussion on a drum segment; overall drumming described as solid.
